success_criteria: Contract gains ExtractInto []string field with yaml/json tag extract_into,omitempty
validate.go rule 11 extends to validate every ExtractInto entry under existing per-entry rules (no traversal, no absolute, no control chars, no drive letters, no UNC, no zero-width)
validate.go gains rule 17 rejecting extract_into entries with code-file extensions (.go, .py, .ts, .tsx, .js, .md, .yaml, .yml, .json, .toml, .rs, .java, .cpp, .h, .c)
PreToolUse hook reads ETHOS_VERIFIER_EXTRACT_INTO and authorizes Write/Edit to non-existing paths under any listed directory; existing path Write/Edit still requires ETHOS_VERIFIER_ALLOWLIST match
SubagentStart sets ETHOS_VERIFIER_EXTRACT_INTO from m.ExtractInto in the verifier env block
Verifier isolation block lists extract_into alongside write_set with prose distinguishing modify vs create

findWriteSetConflicts extends to the six-rule closed form per DES-052 — ws-file/ws-file (existing), ws-file/ws-dir (existing), ws-dir/ws-dir (existing), ws-file/ei-dir is a conflict iff D is a prefix of P (new), ws-dir/ei-dir is a conflict iff segment-prefix overlap (new), ei-dir/ei-dir never conflicts; relation is symmetric
Archetype struct gains ExtractIntoConstraints []string field; archetype.go enforcement applies the same glob rules as WriteSetConstraints
Seed archetypes updated per DES-052 default table (design → docs/**, test → tests/** + *_test.go, investigate → docs/** + .tmp/**, others empty)
lint.go gains lintMonolithPressure and lintExtractIntoFileEntries warnings per DES-052
Tests cover every new code path — per-entry validation, PreToolUse stat branch, SubagentStart env propagation, admission control table rows, archetype constraint enforcement, lint heuristics
make check passes — go vet, staticcheck, shellcheck, markdownlint, all tests pass with -race -count=1
make check-oo does not regress (no decline in OO scores on touched files)
CHANGELOG.md gains an Added entry under
Round-by-round summary captured in the mission reflection so the leader can append it when transitioning DES-052 from DRAFT to SETTLED (DESIGN.md is leader-authored; not in this worker's write_set)

Commit incrementally — one commit per logical step (schema field, then validation, then PreToolUse, then SubagentStart, then admission, then archetypes, then lint, then archetype YAMLs, then CHANGELOG/DESIGN). Each commit must pass make check. Do not accumulate more than 30 minutes of uncommitted changes.
ethos mission dispatch accepts --extract-into flag taking comma-separated relative directories; help text names the flag and links DES-052
Empty/omitted --extract-into leaves Contract.ExtractInto as a nil slice — backward compatible
Populated --extract-into entries flow through splitCSV to Contract.ExtractInto and are validated by the same per-entry rules as write_set (validateWriteSetEntry plus rule 17)

cmd/ethos/mission_test.go gains a subtest exercising dispatch with --extract-into — confirms successful create, the created contract carries ExtractInto, and a malformed entry (code-file extension or traversal) is rejected with a clear error
AGENTS.md mission contract YAML example shows extract_into alongside write_set with a one-line annotation distinguishing modify-existing vs create-new
AGENTS.md dispatch example uses --extract-into alongside --write-set
AGENTS.md gains one short paragraph on the asymmetric semantics — modify-existing-via write_set, create-new-via either axis, never modify-via-extract_into — pointing at DES-052 for the full design
AGENTS.md archetypes section names extract_into_constraints and points at the DES-052 default table in DESIGN.md
CHANGELOG [Unreleased] gains an Added entry for the dispatch flag and an Added entry for the docs alignment
make check clean; make check-oo does not regress
Commit incrementally — one commit per logical step (CLI flag + test, AGENTS.md docs, CHANGELOG). Each commit must pass make check. Do not accumulate more than 30 minutes of uncommitted changes.
Verdict line at the top — APPROVE / ITERATE / REJECT — with one-sentence basis

Every backward-compatibility claim in the Migration section is verified against the existing on-disk format — name one case where an old artifact would fail to round-trip, or confirm the omitempty discipline holds across every new field
Mission ID and delegation ID counter atomicity across 5 concurrent repos is verified — name the failure mode the proposed single-global-counter-file does not catch, or confirm atomicity holds
Migration story for in-flight missions (a mission open at the moment of binary upgrade) is checked — what happens to its lock, its event log, its contract, its delegations
The PostToolUse full-tool_input persistence cost is bounded and reasonable — name the workload that breaks this, or confirm the 5MB-per-long-session estimate is sound
At least one cross-tool compatibility check the DES missed is proposed, or the compatibility surface is confirmed complete
Output written to .tmp/missions/results/des-054-review-rsc.md, no other files modified
Verdict line at the top — APPROVE / ITERATE / REJECT — with one-sentence basis

Each of the four new primitives (delegation, synthesized ad-hoc contract, predicate language, per-delegation flock) is evaluated for whether it earns its keep — name at least one cheaper alternative the DES did not consider, or confirm the four-primitive set is minimal

Pike's habitual question — do we really need this? — is answered explicitly for the predicate language. Four closed predicates vs one fixed predicate vs general expression language — argue for the chosen point
The DES's claim that audited-delegation is one missing concept rather than five separate problems is pressure-tested — name the case where the unification is wrong, or confirm the unification holds
At least one rejected alternative the DES missed is proposed, or the rejected-alternatives section is confirmed exhaustive
Output written to .tmp/missions/results/des-054-review-rop.md, no other files modified
Verdict line at the top — APPROVE / ITERATE / REJECT — with one-sentence basis
The seven pseudo-Z invariants in the DES are each checked for satisfiability — name one invariant that is unsatisfiable under realistic mission shapes, or confirm closure under composition

The audited-delegation chain is verified for transitive closure — a delegation that spawns a sub-delegation that spawns a sub-sub-delegation produces an unbroken parent chain in the audit log; name the case where this breaks, or confirm

The precondition predicate scope rule (preconditions can only reference their own delegation's audit log) is checked against the case where the contract has both a worker mission and a verifier mission — is the audit scope correctly identified
The synthesized ad-hoc contract case is pressure-tested as a formal entity — does it have the same invariants as a leader-authored contract, or does its synthetic origin weaken the invariant set
The race between Stat and Write in extract_into (called out in DES-052) is re-examined under DES-054 — does adding the delegation record reshape the race
Output written to .tmp/missions/results/des-054-review-jra.md, no other files modified

The audited-delegation chain is verified for transitive closure under depth >= 3 — name the case where this breaks, or confirm
The synthesized ad-hoc contract case is pressure-tested as a formal entity — does it have the same invariants as a leader-authored contract
The race between Stat and Write in extract_into is re-examined under DES-054 — does adding the delegation record reshape the race

The review is committed via proper mission ceremony — ethos mission reflect, ethos mission result, ethos mission close — before the agent returns control. Do not assume the leader will submit on your behalf.

Mission close ceremony (reflect → result → close) is performed FIRST before any review-content work, with a minimal viable reflection and result
The prior reviews at m-2026-05-22-001 and m-2026-05-22-004 (your two prior attempts) are read and either endorsed or contradicted — do not paste forward
Any new findings not in the two prior reviews are appended to the artifact AFTER the ceremony has closed the mission
The mission ends in status=closed regardless of how much content is added

v5 dropped counter.yaml in favor of sibling per-namespace per-date files; verify this answers the round-3 file-format-break REQ cleanly
v5 collapsed dual audit-store to single session-level store; verify this preserves the audit invariants
Transition window two minor versions; verify the phase sequencing is consistent

Mission ends status=closed

auditEntry struct gains parent_session, agent_id, agent_type, delegation_id, parent_delegation, contract_id, tool_input (full), tool_input_hash; all new fields are omitempty so old JSONL parses with new code

Store gains a two-root constructor — NewStoreWithRoots(repoRoot, globalRoot) — and dispatches Create / Load / List / Update / Close / LoadResults / LoadReflections / LogReader through both roots; old NewStore(root) preserved as a thin wrapper for backward compat
Mission files write under <repoRoot>/.ethos/missions/<mission-id>/ when repoRoot is set; fall back to <globalRoot>/missions/<id>.yaml shape when repoRoot is empty
Session audit JSONL writes under <repoRoot>/.ethos/sessions/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<session-id>/audit.jsonl when repoRoot is set; legacy ~/.punt-labs/ethos/sessions/<id>.audit.jsonl read as fallback

NewID gains rollback API — returns (id string, release func(commit bool), error); the per-namespace per-date counter file at ~/.punt-labs/ethos/counters/<namespace>-YYYY-MM-DD is a single-int file with flock-guarded read/inc/write; release(false) decrements; release(true) commits; idempotent if another process already incremented
Mission ID and delegation ID counters live in separate sibling files under ~/.punt-labs/ethos/counters/ — missions-YYYY-MM-DD and delegations-YYYY-MM-DD
JSONL atomic-write contract — f.Sync() after every audit-log line; reader tolerates partial trailing lines (no \n) with stderr warning
Contract YAML decoded with KnownFields(true); auditEntry JSONL decoded permissive — the asymmetry is named in a code comment in audit_log.go

Tests cover schema additions (round-trip with and without new fields), Store two-root dispatch (repo vs global), per-namespace counter atomicity under 10-goroutine concurrent NewID calls, JSONL partial-line recovery, audit-entry full-tool_input persistence, tool_input_hash canonical-JSON stability
AGENTS.md storage layout table updated to show the new two-tree layout (missions/<mission-id>/, sessions/<date>-<session-id>/) and sibling counter file pattern
CHANGELOG [Unreleased] Added entry naming DES-054 phase 1
make check clean; make check-oo does not regress on touched files

Commit incrementally — one commit per logical step (auditEntry, Store dispatch, NewID, JSONL atomic-write, tests, docs). Each commit passes make check. Do not accumulate more than 30 minutes of uncommitted changes.
Agent-definition PostToolUse hook command gains a path filter so writes under .tmp/ or .ethos/ exit 0 without firing make check
Hook command structure — case statement with three branches — bypass .tmp/ and .ethos/ paths, then .go/Makefile/.sh/.yaml/.yml run make check, else exit 0
The exact shell expression in the case statement is the one already deployed locally in .claude/agents/bwk.md (verbatim) so the generator emits what manual patching emitted
Tests in generate_agents_test.go assert the emitted hook command contains the .tmp/ and .ethos/ bypass clause and the .go/.sh/.yaml/.yml trigger clause
make check clean — go vet, staticcheck, shellcheck, markdownlint, tests

Commit incrementally — one commit for the generator change + tests. CHANGELOG entry will be added by the leader in a follow-up commit (mission scope excludes CHANGELOG to avoid write-set overlap with concurrent DES-054 phase 1 mission).
All 27 markdownlint errors under .ethos/missions/**/artifacts/*.md are resolved — make check passes on these files
MD040 (fenced code blocks without language) fixed by adding text language tag to bare ```fences
MD004 (list style consistency) fixed where mixed dash and plus list markers exist
Content semantics preserved — no editorial changes; only markdown hygiene
Commit message captures which files were touched and the rationale (markdownlint hygiene for historical review artifacts)
make check clean for the entire repo
